AnalysisExamples.title: Analysis Examples
AnalysisExamples.intro: This is an example on the standard approach to fitting GLM models to spike train data. This data set was obtained at the Society For Neuroscience '08 Workshop on Workshop on Neural Signal Processing Compare to analysis with Neural Spike Analysis Toolbox

AnalysisExamples2.title: Analysis Examples 2
AnalysisExamples2.intro: Compare with traditional Neural Spike Train Analysis here

CovariateExamples.title: Test the Cov class
CovariateExamples.intro: Covariates are just like signals with a mean and a standard deviation They have two representations, the default (original representation) and a zero-mean representation

DecodingExample.title: STIMULUS DECODING
DecodingExample.intro: In this example we show how to decode a univariate and a bivariate stimulus based on a point process observations using nSTAT. Even though due to the simulated nature of the data, we know the exact condition intensity function, we estimate the parameters before moving on to the decoding stage.
headings: Fit a model to the spikedata to obtain a model CIF

DecodingExampleWithHist.title: 1-D Stimulus Decode with History Effect
DecodingExampleWithHist.intro: In the above decoding example, the simulated neurons did not have memory. That is their previous firing activity did not modulate their current probability of firing. In reality the firing history does affect the probabilty of neuronal firing (eg. refractory period, bursting, etc.). In this example, we simulate a population a neurons that exhibit this type of history dependence. We then decode the stimulus activity based on a conditional intensity function that includes the correct history terms and one that assumes no history dependence.

EventsExamples.title: Events
EventsExamples.intro: Events are simple object to use and are aimed to facilitate illustration of epochs in any time of data.

Examples.title: Examples
Examples.intro: Example files are provided for each of the classes defined in the Neural Spike Analysis Toolbox so that the user can understand the functionality of each in more detail.

HippocampalPlaceCellExample.title: HIPPOCAMPAL PLACE CELL - RECEPTIVE FIELD ESTIMATION
HippocampalPlaceCellExample.intro: Estimation of receptive fields of neurons is a very common data analysis problem in neuroscience. Here we use the nSTAT software to perform an estimation of the receptive fields of hippocampal place cells using a bivariate Gaussian model and Zernike polynomials. The number of zernike polynomials is based on "An Analysis of Hippocampal Spatio-Temporal Representations Using a Bayesian Algorithm for Neural Spike Train Decoding" Barbieri et. al 2005. The data used herein in was provided by Dr. Ricardo Barbieri on 2/28/2011.

HistoryExamples.title: Test History
HistoryExamples.intro: Generete a nspikeTrain and define a set of history windows of interest. We desire windows from 1-2ms, 2-3ms, 3-5ms, and 5ms-10ms. The history object with this windows in created below and then the
headings: Example 2: History covariates for a collection of Neural Spikes (nstColl)

HybridFilterExample.title: Hybrid Point Process Filter Example
HybridFilterExample.intro: This example is based on an implementation of the Hybrid Point Process filter described in General-purpose filter design for neural prosthetic devices by Srinivasan L, Eden UT, Mitter SK, Brown EN in J Neurophysiol. 2007 Oct, 98(4):2456-75.

PPSimExample.title: General Point Process Simulation
PPSimExample.intro: In this demo, we show how sample-paths of a point process (PP) can be generated from specification of its conditional intensity function (CIF). We then use the generated PP data to validate the outputs of the Neural Spike Analysis Toolbox.
headings: Results for across all sample paths

SignalObjExamples.title: Using the SignalObj Class
SignalObjExamples.intro: In this file we will give several examples of how the SignalObj can be used. A description of all of the properties of SignalObj can be found at: SignalObj Class Definition
